outcomes.description: Query, create, update, and delete items persisted in the PostgreSQL items table via ItemRepository.

scenarios.description: System receives GET /api/items with optional skip (default 0) and limit (default 100) query parameters. ItemService.get_items delegates to ItemRepository.get_all, which executes db.query(Item).offset(skip).limit(limit).all() against the items table. No authentication guard is applied. Returns List[ItemResponse] with fields: id (int), name (str), description (str), completed (bool), created_at (datetime), updated_at (datetime).

actions.description: FastAPI route handler read_items; query params: skip (int, default 0) — number of records to skip; limit (int, default 100) — maximum records to return. No authentication guard. Response model: List[ItemResponse].

ItemService.get_items(skip, limit) → ItemRepository.get_all(skip, limit) → db.query(Item).offset(skip).limit(limit).all(); table: items; SQLAlchemy ORM; columns: id, name, description, completed, created_at, updated_at; indexes: ix_items_name_desc (name, description), ix_item_status_time (completed, updated_at).

FastAPI auto-serializes ORM Item objects to List[ItemResponse] via Pydantic. Response fields: id (int), name (str), description (str), completed (bool), created_at (datetime), updated_at (datetime). Config: from_attributes = True.

branchesAndErrors.what: no branches or error paths in GET /api/items handler
branchesAndErrors.handledAs: noted in audit.warnings — handler delegates to repo.get_all with no guard conditions
